# Claude Code — Best Practices

<!-- Generated from hub/ by agents/compile. Do not edit this file. -->
<!-- Run `bun run compile` to regenerate. -->

## Prompting

- **Reference files with @ syntax instead of describing their contents** — Use `@path/to/file` in prompts and CLAUDE.md so Claude reads the file directly; do not paraphrase or inline file contents. ([source](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/best-practices#provide-rich-content))
- **Scope tasks with specific files, patterns, and symptoms** — Replace vague prompts with ones that name files, point to reference patterns, and describe symptoms — precision shrinks the correction loop. ([source](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/best-practices#provide-specific-context-in-your-prompts))

## CLI

- **Run /clear between unrelated tasks** — Reset the context window with `/clear` when switching tasks and after two failed corrections on the same issue — a fresh prompt beats a cluttered one. ([source](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/best-practices#manage-context-aggressively))
- **Resume prior conversations with --continue and --resume** — Use `claude --continue` to pick up the latest session or `claude --resume` to pick from recent ones; name sessions with `/rename` so you can find them. ([source](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/best-practices#resume-conversations))

## Workflows

- **Use Plan Mode before multi-file changes** — Enter Plan Mode for any change that spans multiple files or unfamiliar code; skip it when the diff fits in one sentence. ([source](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/best-practices#explore-first-then-plan-then-code))
- **Give Claude a way to verify its own work** — Always provide an automated check (tests, linter, expected output, screenshot compare) so Claude can confirm its work without relying on you as the sole loop. ([source](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/best-practices#give-claude-a-way-to-verify-its-work))

## Best Practices

- **Keep CLAUDE.md under 200 lines** — Target under 200 lines per CLAUDE.md; longer files burn context and drop in adherence — split overflow into `.claude/rules/` or @-imported files. ([source](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/memory#write-effective-instructions))
- **Use path-scoped .claude/rules/ files in large projects** — For rules that only apply to certain files, put them in `.claude/rules/*.md` with a `paths:` glob frontmatter — they load only when relevant. ([source](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/memory#organize-rules-with-claude/rules/))
